frameworks.name: connectomics
frameworks.one_line: Volumetric EM reconstruction to map synaptic wiring at cell-type resolution.

frameworks.name: classical neuroanatomy of inhibition
frameworks.one_line: Compartment-targeting taxonomy extended with single-cell connectivity data.

frameworks.name: cortical circuit motifs
frameworks.one_line: Inhibitory circuits described as coordinated motif groups across cell types.

core_question: What are the cell-type-specific connectivity rules of inhibitory neurons across all layers of mouse visual cortex?
key_assumptions: Dendritic compartment targeting defines meaningful inhibitory cell classes
Dense EM segmentation captures cell-type-resolved wiring
Excitatory neurons can be classified from dendritic morphology and synaptic input maps
manipulations: Cell-type identity of presynaptic inhibitory neurons
Dendritic compartment of postsynaptic targets (perisomatic vs dendritic)
Excitatory subpopulation identity among spatially intermingled cells
key_findings: Wiring diagram of >70,000 inhibitory synapses across 1,352 cells spanning all cortical layers
A disinhibitory specialist class preferentially targets basket cells
Many interneurons differentially target spatially intermingled excitatory subpopulations
Inhibitory connectivity is organized into motif groups co-targeting perisomatic and dendritic compartments
interpretations: Inhibitory circuitry exhibits widespread cell-type-specific targeting beyond classical compartment rules
Motif groups coordinate complementary inhibition onto shared excitatory targets
